text.title: Contract-First Development Workflow
text.summary: Six-phase workflow from spec design to deployment, with CI integration rules.

list.item: Design: write the OpenAPI spec for the new endpoint or resource before any code.
Review: treat the spec change as a PR — require approval from at least one consumer team.
Generate: produce server stubs, client SDKs, and test scaffolding from the spec via openapi-generator.
Implement: fill in business logic in the generated stubs; do not edit generated model files directly.
Validate: run openapi-core against the live server in CI to confirm responses match the spec.
Deploy: the spec is source of truth; deployment gates on passing contract tests.

rule.statement: The OpenAPI spec is the source of truth. Implementation must conform to the spec; the spec is never updated to match a drifted implementation.
rule.rationale: Allowing spec-to-implementation drift invalidates generated clients and documentation. Once consumers rely on the spec, breaking it silently causes integration failures.

rule.statement: Treat spec changes that break existing responses (remove fields, change types) as breaking changes requiring a version bump.
rule.rationale: Additive changes (new optional fields) are backwards-compatible. Removals and type changes break existing clients silently.

rule.statement: Run Spectral lint on every PR that touches the spec file; fail the build on errors.
rule.rationale: Spectral catches missing operationIds, undocumented error responses, and naming violations before review — issues that are cheap to fix early and expensive later.

rule.statement: Run contract tests (openapi-core response validation) on every commit; do not merge if responses diverge from the spec.
rule.rationale: Contract tests are the only automated check that the running implementation still matches the published spec.

text.reference: GitHub Actions workflow for spec lint and contract test
